text.title: Spatial Accessibility Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding spatial accessibility audits. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every spatial interaction MUST support at least 2 independent input modalities chosen from {gaze+dwell, voice, single-hand, two-hand, controller, switch}. If hand-tracking is primary, the second modality MUST NOT be gaze+dwell alone.

rationale.source: W3C XAUR — XR Accessibility User Requirements

rule.rationale: Motor limitations, gloves, controllers-only environments, and hand-tracking failures all eliminate single-modality paths. Two independent modalities ensure at least one remains viable.

rule.statement: Every critical user flow MUST have a non-spatial fallback path: flat-window mode, companion app, or voice-only invocation. Skip-conditions MUST be documented per flow.
rule.rationale: Users unable to tolerate immersion or lacking room-scale space cannot be excluded from core functionality without an alternative path.

rule.statement: Every interaction that works in standing mode MUST also work seated. UI MUST reposition to seated eye-height (1.0-1.2 m above ground) when seated mode is active; required upward gaze MUST NOT exceed 20 degrees above horizontal.
rule.rationale: Enterprise XR, rehabilitation contexts, and users with mobility impairments require seated parity. Sustained upward head tilt causes neck fatigue within minutes.

rule.statement: Locomotion MUST offer BOTH vignette and teleport options; smooth-only locomotion is forbidden. Snap-turn and smooth-turn MUST both be available. Defaults are platform-recommended but user-overridable.

rationale.source: Meta Quest Documentation — comfort best practices

rule.rationale: Motion-sickness triggers differ per user; no single solution fixes nausea universally. Configurable options are the only reliable accommodation.

rule.statement: Spatial captions MUST default to head-locked subtitles at ~3 m apparent distance. A world-locked option with size + contrast slider MUST be available. Audio descriptions MUST be provided for visually-encoded meaning.
rule.rationale: Head-locked captions are readable during motion; world-locked captions are immersive but unreadable in motion. Defaults favour readability; override favours immersion.

rule.statement: Before declaring spatial accessibility compliance, hardware testing MUST include ≥1 low-vision, ≥1 motor-impaired, and ≥1 motion-sensitive tester. Synthetic-only testing is forbidden as compliance evidence.
rule.rationale: No automated tool can substitute for real user validation in spatial contexts. Comfort settings that "feel fine" to designers routinely fail real users with vestibular disorders.
